items.headline: USMNT beat Bosnia 2-0 — first World Cup knockout win since 2002
items.body: The U.S. are into the last 16. Folarin Balogun struck right before halftime and Malik Tillman curled in a beauty of a free kick to make it 2-0 — and they held on even after Balogun was sent off in the 64th, finishing the night with 10 men. It's their first knockout-round win at a World Cup since 2002, and Belgium is up next in Seattle on Monday.
items.say: The U.S. beat Bosnia 2-0 last night and made the last 16 — first World Cup knockout win since 2002, and they did it a man down. Belgium's next on Monday.

items.headline: Ronaldo vs. Modrić, one last time — Portugal meet Croatia tonight
items.body: One of these legends is playing his final World Cup match tonight. Cristiano Ronaldo (41) and Luka Modrić (40) have six Ballon d'Ors between them (five for Ronaldo) and are almost certainly at their last tournament. It's the first-ever World Cup meeting between Portugal and Croatia, in Toronto — winner into the Round of 16, loser heads home for good.
items.say: Ronaldo vs. Modrić tonight at the World Cup, and it's win-or-go-home — so one of them is playing his last-ever World Cup game. Kind of the end of an era either way.

links.text: ESPN: 'Icons' Modrić and Ronaldo battle to avoid a send-off
Al Jazeera: Portugal vs. Croatia preview

items.headline: Lamine Yamal is finally fully fit — Spain start their knockout run today
items.body: Spain's 18-year-old superstar has been easing back from a hamstring injury, but the coach says he's 100% now and ready to start against Austria in L.A. Yamal already has a goal at this World Cup, and the favorites will want the most exciting teenager in the game firing as the knockouts begin. Opta gives Spain about a 70% chance to advance.
items.say: Spain play today and their 18-year-old wonderkid Lamine Yamal is finally fully fit — honestly the most fun player in the world to watch right now. Worth catching.

links.text: ESPN: Spain's plan for Austria — get the ball to Yamal
Al Jazeera: Spain vs. Austria preview

items.headline: Whoa — Celtics trade Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George
items.body: Nobody saw this one coming. Boston sent Jaylen Brown — the 2024 Finals MVP, fresh off the best year of his career — to Philadelphia for Paul George and a bundle of draft picks. After 10 seasons in green, Brown now suits up for one of Boston's biggest rivals. And that's just one domino: Kawhi Leonard got dealt back to Toronto too. Wild week.
items.say: The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown — their Finals MVP — to the Sixers for Paul George. Absolutely nobody saw that coming. NBA free agency has been total chaos.

links.text: ESPN: trade grades — who won the blockbuster?

items.headline: Serena's emotional return ends in a three-set loss — and a knee scare
items.body: Playing her first singles match in nearly four years, a 44-year-old Serena Williams pushed 20-year-old Aussie Maya Joint to three sets on Centre Court before falling 6-3, 6-7(6), 6-3, with her two daughters cheering her on. She was still cracking 120-mph serves — but tweaked her knee, which now puts her planned doubles run alongside sister Venus in doubt.
items.say: Serena played her first singles match in four years at Wimbledon and lost a tight three-setter — still hitting 120-mph serves at 44. Now there's a knee worry hanging over her doubles with Venus.

links.text: Wimbledon: Serena v Maya Joint highlights
Olympics.com: valiant Serena falls in three

items.headline: 'Enola Holmes 3' just dropped on Netflix — the platform's most-anticipated of 2026
items.body: Millie Bobby Brown is back as the teenage detective, and this one's a ride: mid-wedding to Lord Tewkesbury, Enola learns her brother Sherlock (Henry Cavill) has been kidnapped, sending her chasing the case across Malta. It landed July 1 and was billed as Netflix's most-anticipated film of the year. Easy, breezy weekend watch.
items.say: The new Enola Holmes just hit Netflix — Millie Bobby Brown's back, and this time Sherlock gets kidnapped right at her wedding. Perfect couch movie.

items.headline: 'Silo' returns for Season 3 on Apple TV+ this Friday
items.body: One of the best sci-fi shows going is back July 3. Rebecca Ferguson's Juliette survived her 'cleaning' and returns with memory loss as the underground silo reels from a rebellion — plus a new origin story set centuries earlier. Ten episodes, dropping weekly through September, and Apple has already ordered a fourth and final season.
items.say: Silo's back Friday on Apple TV — that underground-bunker sci-fi one. If you haven't started it, it's a great binge to pick up.

items.headline: Essence Fest takes over New Orleans this Fourth of July weekend
items.body: The country's biggest celebration of Black music and culture runs July 3-5 in New Orleans, and the Superdome lineup is stacked: Cardi B, Kehlani and Latto open Friday, Brandy & Monica reunite to headline Saturday alongside Patti LaBelle, and George Clinton & Parliament-Funkadelic close it out Sunday. Michelle Obama even kicks off the weekend with a live conversation.
items.say: Essence Fest is on in New Orleans this weekend — Cardi B, Patti LaBelle, and Brandy and Monica reuniting on stage. That's a heck of a Fourth of July lineup.

links.text: Brandy & Monica — 'The Boy Is Mine' (official video)

items.headline: Everyone's buzzing about Nolan's 'The Odyssey' — out July 17
items.body: Christopher Nolan's mythic epic — Homer's 'Odyssey' shot entirely on brand-new IMAX film cameras — is two weeks out, and the hype is real (demand for early tickets literally crashed AMC's app). The cast is absurd: Matt Damon as Odysseus, plus Tom Holland, Zendaya, Robert Pattinson, Charlize Theron and Lupita Nyong'o. This is the summer's big-screen event.
items.say: You seen the trailer for Nolan's Odyssey? Out July 17 — Matt Damon, Tom Holland, Zendaya, the whole cast. People are already going nuts trying to get IMAX tickets.
